metadata.title: Set Up React Context Providers for State Management

story.soThat: global state is managed consistently across all components without prop drilling
story.tasks: - Implement AppContext with reducer pattern (useReducer with AppReducer) - Implement SessionContext with session management (START_SESSION, END_SESSION, PAUSE_SESSION, RESUME_SESSION) - Implement UserSettingsContext with localStorage persistence - Wrap App.tsx with all three providers in correct order (UserSettingsContext → AppContext → SessionContext → Router) - Create test component to verify reading and dispatching to each context - Define TypeScript types for all state and actions

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
**Given** the Dexie database is operational (Story 1.4 complete) **When** I implement the Context providers **Then** the following context providers are created:
**AppContext** (src/context/AppContext.tsx): - State: streak: number, onlineStatus: boolean, lastSyncTimestamp: string | null - Actions: SET_STREAK, UPDATE_ONLINE_STATUS, SET_LAST_SYNC - useReducer pattern with AppReducer
**SessionContext** (src/context/SessionContext.tsx): - State: currentModule: string | null, sessionId: string | null, sessionStatus: SessionStatus, startTime: string | null - Actions: START_SESSION, END_SESSION, PAUSE_SESSION, RESUME_SESSION
**UserSettingsContext** (src/context/UserSettingsContext.tsx): - State: reducedMotion: boolean, soundEnabled: boolean, dailyGoalMinutes: number, researchModeEnabled: boolean - Actions: UPDATE_SETTINGS - Persists to localStorage on every change
**And** src/App.tsx wraps the app with all three providers in correct order **And** A test component successfully reads and dispatches to each context **And** TypeScript types are fully defined for all state and actions

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Foundation & Core Refactor
doc.snippet: Defines AppState, AppAction, SessionState, SessionAction, SessionStatus types. AppContext manages streak, onlineStatus, lastSyncTimestamp. SessionContext manages currentModule, sessionId, sessionStatus, startTime. UserSettingsContext manages reducedMotion, soundEnabled, dailyGoalMinutes, researchModeEnabled with localStorage persistence.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Foundation & Core Refactor
doc.snippet: Context provider wrapping order in App.tsx: UserSettingsContextProvider (load persisted settings first) → AppContextProvider (initialize app-level state) → SessionContextProvider (manage active sessions) → BrowserRouter → Routes. This order ensures settings are available before app state initializes.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Foundation & Core Refactor
doc.snippet: useApp() returns {state, dispatch, setStreak, updateOnlineStatus, setLastSync}. useSession() returns {state, dispatch, startSession, endSession, pauseSession, resumeSession}. useUserSettings() returns {settings, updateSettings}. All hooks provide both raw dispatch and convenience methods.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Foundation & Core Refactor
doc.snippet: STORAGE_KEYS constants: STREAK, LAST_SESSION_DATE, USER_SETTINGS, LAST_USED_MODULE, RESEARCH_MODE_ENABLED. All keys namespaced with 'discalculas:' prefix. UserSettings interface: reducedMotion, soundEnabled, dailyGoalMinutes, researchModeEnabled (all typed).

doc.title: Architecture Specification
doc.snippet: React Context chosen over Redux for simplicity and local-first architecture. Three context providers: AppContext (global app state), SessionContext (active training session), UserSettingsContext (persisted preferences). LocalStorage keys: STREAK, LAST_SESSION_DATE, USER_SETTINGS, LAST_USED_MODULE, RESEARCH_MODE_ENABLED.

doc.title: Architecture Specification
doc.snippet: Context providers live in src/context/ folder: AppContext.tsx, SessionContext.tsx, UserSettingsContext.tsx. Each exports the provider component and custom hook for consuming the context.

artifact.reason: Main application component that needs to be wrapped with all three Context providers. Currently renders design system demo. Must wrap with UserSettingsContext → AppContext → SessionContext → Router per architecture spec.

Dexie database implementation from Story 1.4 (prerequisite). Provides 8 tables including sessions, assessments, telemetry_logs. Context providers will use this for data persistence alongside localStorage.
Database health check utility that tests read/write operations. Can be used in Context providers to verify IndexedDB accessibility before initializing state.

node.purpose: IndexedDB wrapper for session/telemetry data persistence
React hooks for live queries (future integration with contexts)

story-context.constraints: - Context provider wrapping order is MANDATORY: UserSettingsContext (outermost) → AppContext → SessionContext → BrowserRouter (innermost). This order ensures settings load first, then app state, then session state. - Must use useReducer pattern with TypeScript-typed actions for AppContext and SessionContext (not useState). - All Context state and action types must be exported for type safety. - UserSettingsContext MUST persist to localStorage on every UPDATE_SETTINGS action. - Export custom hooks (useApp, useSession, useUserSettings) - do NOT export raw Context objects. - Follow reducer pattern from architecture.md communication-patterns section. - Prepare for future WebSocket/sync in AppContext (add commented placeholders). - All localStorage keys must use STORAGE_KEYS constants with 'discalculas:' namespace. - Handle corrupt/missing localStorage data gracefully with default fallbacks. - TypeScript strict mode must pass with zero errors.

interface.name: AppState
interface.signature: { streak: number; onlineStatus: boolean; lastSyncTimestamp: string | null }

interface.name: AppAction
interface.signature: { type: 'SET_STREAK'; payload: number } | { type: 'UPDATE_ONLINE_STATUS'; payload: boolean } | { type: 'SET_LAST_SYNC'; payload: string | null }

interface.name: SessionState
interface.signature: { currentModule: string | null; sessionId: string | null; sessionStatus: SessionStatus; startTime: string | null }

interface.name: SessionAction
interface.signature: { type: 'START_SESSION'; payload: { module: string; sessionId: string } } | { type: 'END_SESSION' } | { type: 'PAUSE_SESSION' } | { type: 'RESUME_SESSION' }

interface.name: UserSettings
interface.signature: { reducedMotion: boolean; soundEnabled: boolean; dailyGoalMinutes: number; researchModeEnabled: boolean }

interface.name: useApp hook
interface.signature: () => { state: AppState; dispatch: Dispatch<AppAction>; setStreak: (n: number) => void; updateOnlineStatus: (b: boolean) => void; setLastSync: (s: string | null) => void }

interface.name: useSession hook
interface.signature: () => { state: SessionState; dispatch: Dispatch<SessionAction>; startSession: (module: string, sessionId: string) => void; endSession: () => void; pauseSession: () => void; resumeSession: () => void }

tests.standards: Unit and integration tests required using Vitest + React Testing Library. 100% coverage threshold enforced (statements, branches, functions, lines). Co-located test files (*.test.tsx) next to each Context. AAA pattern mandatory (Arrange, Act, Assert). Use user-centric queries (getByRole, getByLabelText). Test both raw dispatch and convenience methods. Test localStorage persistence in UserSettingsContext.

test.description: Verify AppContext updates streak and persists to localStorage

ideas.test: Unit test: Render test component that uses useApp(), dispatch SET_STREAK action, assert state.streak updated and localStorage contains value.
Unit test: Use useSession() hook, call startSession('training', 'session-123'), assert state.sessionStatus === 'active', call endSession(), assert state.sessionStatus === 'completed'.

test.description: Verify UserSettingsContext persists on every change

ideas.test: Integration test: Use useUserSettings(), call updateSettings({soundEnabled: false}), assert localStorage updated immediately, reload component, assert setting persisted.

test.description: Verify context provider wrapping order in App.tsx

ideas.test: Integration test: Render App component, verify all contexts are accessible from a test child component, verify no context is undefined.

test.description: Verify TypeScript types compile with strict mode

ideas.test: Meta test: Run 'npx tsc --noEmit', verify zero errors related to Context files.

test.description: Verify corrupt localStorage data falls back to defaults

ideas.test: Unit test: Pre-populate localStorage with invalid JSON, render UserSettingsContext, assert default settings loaded without crash.
